#Summary1=(human1) Two people talk about meal prep and meal planning, which are a convenient way for busy people to prepare for the week.
#Text=Meal prep is basically you 're prepping for the rest of your week .
#Text=Especially for people that are parents or people that have very busy lives , it 's really good to pick a day that you 're either off or less busy and prep everything for the week .
#Text=Because if you think about just one meal , if you 're making something as simple as , let 's say , spaghetti , you put onions , ground beef , pasta , maybe fresh tomatoes .
#Text=Whatever you put in it , you have to cut the onion , cut the tomato .
#Text=Just think , if you , several days prior you had cut , let 's say four onions , now you do n't have to sit there and spend , you know , five minutes doing that , it 's already all done .
#Text=It sounds like you 're getting a lot of the tedious stuff out of the way so that when you go to prepare the meal , the ingredients is already prepped .
#Text=Absolutely .
#Text=So meal prepping and meal plans , they kind of go hand in hand .
#Text=What I do exactly is before I go grocery shopping , before I even make a grocery list , I sit and I make a whole entire list of maybe between 15 to probably 20 meals .
#Text=In one month you 'll probably eat at least one thing twice , whether you 're eating it as leftovers or you 're just making it twice .
#Text=It 's really good to make a whole entire list , and then from that list of meals , you then make your grocery list and you always duplicate .
#Text=So if you make several things with onions , obviously you do n't need one onion , you need several onions or potatoes or whatever you use for that meal .
#Text=For me , I like to --
#Text=So Sunday is my day that I do food prep , chores , whatever .
#Text=That 's the day that I do my things .
#Text=I will prep for the week and that is the best advice I can tell most people .
#Text=If you 're not freezing these prepped items , it 's best to do for the week because that keeps things from going bad .
#Text=It 's not easy to be like , okay , Wednesday I 'm making tacos , Thursday I 'm making spaghetti , Friday -- because you do n't know how much you 're gon na eat that week .
#Text=And so it 's kind of like you go as you go .
#Text=So if you come home and it 's really late at night and you want to grab that freezer meal that you happen to make , then that 's great .
#Text=But if you want to sit and make , like I said , tacos or whatever , and everything is already prepped and you just have to either throw it in the microwave or throw it in a pan .
#Text=I call those dump meals when you throw it all in one pan and then it 's done .
#Text=So that 's what I do throughout the week to make my life easier throughout the week .
#Text=When you 're working between 8 and 12 hours a day , it 's really good to have everything planned in advance .
#Text=So it 's meal prep and meal plan .
#Text=Absolutely .
#Text=They definitely go hand in hand .
#Text=I mean , of course you can just do food prep if that 's your thing and you do n't want to do the whole meal plan .
#Text=My personal opinion is meal planning and meal prepping go hand in hand because if you 're prepping the food , what are you prepping it for ?
#Text=Your plan , you 're prepping it for a meal that you have planned .
#Text=Um , cut vegetables no longer than a week in advance .
#Text=Because onions release a gas when you cut them , it 's best to leave them in the refrigerator .
#Text=Do n't put them on the counter .
#Text=Yes , they will be fine , but they release a gas which will make them go bad faster .
#Text=Same thing with apples .
#Text=Apples turn brown .
#Text=Same things with potatoes .
#Text=They 'll turn brown .
#Text=So you have to pick and choose .
#Text=So I recommend , let 's say you 're doing a , uh , potato casserole , okay , unless you 're willing to freeze said casserole , I do n't recommend you cutting them for the week .
#Text=Because if you cut them , by the time you use them , even though they technically have n't gone bad , who wants to eat a brown looking potato ?
#Text=Like it 's not pretty .
#Text=It goes with that , that feast with your eyes and then with your mouth kind of thing .
#Text=Exactly .

#Text=Like , even though there have been tips and tricks , they say like , oh , if you get the potatoes and put them in water after they 're cut , or if you do this , who wants to have a bowl of water with potatoes in their fridge ?
#Text=I do n't .
#Text=Some people might .
#Text=And then that changes the starch and all that other stuff .
#Text=So , my advice for certain vegetables , you have to do your own research , um , which ones are best frozen .
#Text=I preferably do n't like frozen green beans or frozen squash or frozen zucchini .
#Text=Potatoes do well , very , very well , frozen .
#Text=So I , um , in my household , we like a lot of , like , dump meals .
